analysis.bounded_question: What is the smallest honest Ratchet-native slice we can keep from the lev autodev family without importing recurring execution ownership?
analysis.recommended_follow_on_shape: only a later bounded validation-boundary or loop-state probe if this audit remains clean
analysis.smallest_honest_slice: one bounded repo-held audit over loop shape, execution/validation separation, exit-condition discipline, and explicit forbid lines

non_goal_hygiene.evidence: candidate request does not widen into runtime ownership or background-service control

gate.reason: current promotion truth, source refs, and non-goal fences support one bounded loop-shape audit only

keep: separation of zero-cost scan from token-spend execution as a pattern only

adapt_away_from: hygiene proposal generation as a live control-plane replacement
keep: priority waterfall over sweep, validate, execute, and bounded hygiene
explicit stop conditions instead of infinite autonomy claims
entity lifecycle vocabulary for later bounded follow-ons
ready / needs_validation / validated state language as background only

adapt_away_from: session init / next / record / validate control
keep: prompt-stack staging as external background context only

cron_or_service_scheduling.why: cron and launchd behavior are not admissible in the first Ratchet-native audit

execution_validation_split.why: the separation between work execution and later validation is the strongest reusable shape in the source family

exit_and_circuit_breakers.why: explicit bounded exit conditions are reusable without importing heartbeat runtime continuity

git_close_loop.why: git pull/push control is not part of this operator

heartbeat_runtime_continuity.why: useful as a later pattern source, but too runtime-coupled for this first slice

priority_tick_waterfall.why: the sweep / validate / execute ordering is valuable, but hygiene generation must stay bounded and non-owning

non_goals: No cron scheduling or recurring tick creation.
No heartbeat process or `lev loop autodev` runtime ownership.
No prompt-stack session control or external plugin runtime import.
No git pull/push, launchd service setup, or background runner claim.
No claim that Ratchet now has a live autodev loop.
recommended_actions: Keep this slice audit-only, repo-held, and nonoperative.
Use the report to scope a later validation-boundary or exit-condition follow-on only if the gate stays clean.
Do not widen this slice into cron, heartbeat runtime, prompt-stack control, `.lev` ownership, git sync, or service installation.
recommended_next_action: Keep this slice audit-only, repo-held, and nonoperative.

cron_scheduler.why: first Ratchet slice must not create or own recurring cron ticks

git_sync.why: git pull/push after successful ticks is explicitly outside the bounded audit

heartbeat_process.source: autodev-lev skill and core-sdlc autodev command
heartbeat_process.why: in-process continuity, sleep pacing, and loop ownership exceed the current bounded audit scope

launchd_service.why: background service installation is not admissible in the first imported autodev slice

plan_surface_ownership.why: Ratchet should not import `.lev/pm/plans/` lifecycle ownership as part of the first slice

prompt_stack_runtime.source: stack skill and autodev stackId wiring
prompt_stack_runtime.why: prompt-stack references are execution background, not a Ratchet-owned runtime seam here
